title: RSF-SPLM-N Launch Major Assault on Dilling, South Kordofan; Residential Areas Shelled
detail: RSF and SPLM-N (al-Hilu faction) launched a major coordinated offensive against Dilling, the second-largest city in South Kordofan, beginning the morning of March 28. Supported by South Sudanese mercenaries, the allied forces conducted intensive drone and heavy artillery strikes on residential neighborhoods, health centres, schools, and crowded markets. The SAF stated it repelled the assault, claiming destruction of 36 combat vehicles and seizure of 4 others in 'fierce' battles. SAF drones struck RSF reinforcements near Habila and along the Dilling–North Kordofan highway. The allied force simultaneously seized the al-Tukma area approximately 7 km east of Dilling and cut the road connecting Dilling to Habila and onward to North Kordofan, causing basic commodities to disappear from local markets.

media.caption: RSF-SPLM-N launch major assault on Dilling, South Kordofan

title: 425+ Families Flee Geissan (Blue Nile) Amid RSF-SPLM-N Military Buildup; Roseires Dam Fears Grow
detail: At least 425 families fled Geissan district in Blue Nile State on March 27–28 as RSF-SPLM-N forces built up military pressure on the area, five days after the fall of Kurmuk. The Emergency Chamber in Geissan called for urgent intervention, including safe humanitarian corridors. Geissan is approximately 200 km southeast of Ed Damazin, the state capital, and its fall would directly threaten both Ed Damazin and the Roseires Dam — Sudan's second-largest hydroelectric facility providing electricity to much of central Sudan. Displaced families faced severe shortages of food, shelter, and basic services upon arrival. Total displacement to Ed Damazin since Eid al-Fitr reached over 73,000.

media.caption: 425+ families flee Geissan amid RSF-SPLM-N military buildup in Blue Nile

title: SAF Drone Strikes Continue on Zalingei, Central Darfur; IDP Camp Areas Hit
detail: SAF drone strikes on Zalingei, Central Darfur continued on March 28, following the evening attacks of March 27. The RSF civil administration in Central Darfur condemned the strikes on residential buildings and the Khamsa Daqaiq IDP camp area. The repeated targeting of Zalingei — Central Darfur's RSF-occupied state capital and a major displacement hub — drew condemnation from humanitarian organizations. Zalingei hosts tens of thousands of displaced persons who fled RSF violence in surrounding areas.

media.caption: SAF drone strikes continue on Zalingei IDP camp in Central Darfur
